prevalence.value: ~0.4-1% of adults; higher in women with amenorrhea/infertility

burden.dalyPerCapita: low population burden; reproductive and sexual health impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Elevated prolactin on repeat testing; exclude pregnancy and hypothyroidism; assess macroprolactin; pituitary MRI

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with dopamine agonists when adherence is good

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care referral for endocrine workup and imaging
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dopamine agonists (cabergoline or bromocriptine); surgery for refractory macroadenomas
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited MRI access and specialist availability in some SUS regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear SBEM guidance and availability of low-cost dopamine agonists
